---
title: "Beyond the Campus: Mapping University Community Travel Patterns and Urban Destinations"
author: "Group X"
date: "April 2026"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
    fig_width: 8
    fig_height: 5
    dev: cairo_pdf
fontsize: 11pt
geometry: margin = 1in
---

<!-- This file serves as both the final paper and a playground for pipeline testing.
     Section markers delimit each part's territory — only edit your own section.
     See CLAUDE.md for the full editing workflow rules. -->

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
# === Packages ===
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# === Project paths ===
PROJ           <- normalizePath("..")
data_dir       <- file.path(PROJ, "data")
od_data_dir    <- file.path(PROJ, "data/OD_Data")
od_subset_dir  <- file.path(PROJ, "data/od_subset")
node_subset_dir <- file.path(PROJ, "data/node_subset")
figures_dir    <- file.path(PROJ, "figures")

dir.create(figures_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# === Shared theme and color scheme ===
project_colors <- c(
  nus_blue    = "#003D7C",
  ntu_orange  = "#E85D04",
  smu_red     = "#C8102E",
  sutd_blue   = "#1B3A6B",
  bus_red     = "#D62828",
  mrt_green   = "#2D6A4F",
  buffer_blue = "#A2D2FF",
  buffer_yel  = "#FFBA08"
)

university_colors <- c(
  NUS  = "#003D7C",
  NTU  = "#E85D04",
  SMU  = "#C8102E",
  SUTD = "#1B3A6B"
)

map_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption  = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 9)
  )

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
```

# Data Loading Reference

<!-- This chunk demonstrates how to load each Part's expected inputs.
     Use it as a reference when building your pipeline. -->

```{r data-loading-reference, eval=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Part 1 outputs
station_mapping   <- read_csv(file.path(data_dir, "station_mapping.csv"),
                             show_col_types = FALSE)
sg_bus_stops      <- read_csv(file.path(data_dir, "sg_bus_stops_all.csv"),
                             show_col_types = FALSE)
sg_mrt_stations   <- read_csv(file.path(data_dir, "sg_mrt_stations_all.csv"),
                             show_col_types = FALSE)

# OD subset — available after re-extraction for 4 universities (Aug 2024 - Aug 2025)
# Example: load one month of bus OD
od_bus_202408 <- read_csv(
  file.path(od_subset_dir, "origin_destination_bus_202408.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Overture POI data (TBD)
# overture_pois <- read_csv(file.path(data_dir, "overture_pois/overture_pois_2025-12-17.csv"))

# Part 2 outputs — available after LDA analysis
# station_topic_classification <- read_csv(...)
# lda_model                     <- readRDS(...)
```

<!-- ===== PART 1: DATA & STATION MAPPING ===== -->
<!-- Part 1 owns: this section, the setup chunk, and Method — Data subsection -->
<!-- 4 universities: NUS, NTU, SMU, SUTD -->
<!-- Identify bus/MRT stations within 500m of each campus -->
<!-- Extract OD subsets for Aug 2024 - Aug 2025 -->

<!-- ===== END PART 1 ===== -->

<!-- ===== PART 2: POI & URBAN FUNCTION IDENTIFICATION ===== -->
<!-- Overture Maps POI → LDA topic modeling → station classification -->
<!-- Single snapshot (Dec 2025) for LDA -->
<!-- Checkpoint: POI quality map, K selection plot, station classification map -->

<!-- ===== END PART 2 ===== -->

<!-- ===== PART 3: TEMPORAL TRAVEL PATTERNS ===== -->
<!-- Monthly OD analysis: Aug 2024 - Aug 2025 -->
<!-- Academic calendar periods: semester (Aug-Nov, Jan-Apr), exam (Dec, May), vacation (Jun-Jul) -->
<!-- Intra-day analysis: morning/afternoon/evening -->
<!-- Checkpoint: monthly trip volumes with period shading -->

<!-- ===== END PART 3 ===== -->

<!-- ===== PART 4: COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS ===== -->
<!-- Inter-university comparison of travel patterns -->
<!-- Destination function analysis (cross-reference with LDA) -->
<!-- Travel distance, catchment area, statistical tests -->
<!-- Checkpoint: radar chart, Sankey diagram -->

<!-- ===== END PART 4 ===== -->

<!-- ===== PART 5: PAPER ASSEMBLY ===== -->
<!-- Part 5 owns: YAML header, Abstract, Introduction, Literature Review,
     Method (except Data subsection), Conclusion, References -->
<!-- TODO: Weave literature contributions, write narrative sections -->

<!-- ===== END PART 5 ===== -->
